body_txt: A ship, facility, city, or some other location used to have a staff numbering in the hundreds, thousands, or tens of thousands. Now it's lucky to hit double digits, and the people available probably missed the orientation video. There are almost as many reasons for the drastic reduction in staff as there are examples, ranging from benign to apocalyptic, but generally the short staff themselves are either a few original members that somehow missed or were spared by the circumstances or newcomers that arrived after a period of abandonment. Time may be spent trying to get equipment back up and running, keeping malfunctions from progressing, or looking for information on the reason for the current situation. This can lead to tension and danger, since there aren't enough people to do everything, and some of those things are probably keeping them from dying. Compare/contrast Oddly Small Organization and Crew of One . May be the result of a Brain Drain , or of having No Budget to hire more people. Can be caused by Mook Depletion . May result in characters doing jobs they're not fully qualified for because they're the Closest Thing We Got . Often a justification for The Main Characters Do Everything . Has nothing to do with the trope Skeleton Crew , since even though that name often refers to this kind of thing, it involves actual skeletons.
